# Alexander Traykov

> Alexander Traykov is a product designer and UX team lead at Quickbase. He designs AI products, enterprise tools, and design systems, and often builds working prototypes to test an idea.

## Primary Pages
- [Homepage](https://traykov.cc): Main portfolio page with selected product stories.
- Case studies: Preview cards are visible on the homepage. Full case studies are coming soon.
- [About](https://traykov.cc/about/): Background, work experience, and public social links.
- [ASCII Shader Playground](https://traykov.cc/playground): Interactive shader and 3D ASCII turntable playground.
- [ASCII Shader Explainer](https://traykov.cc/shader-explainer/): Visual explanation of the shader-to-glyph rendering pipeline.
- [llms.txt](https://traykov.cc/llms.txt): LLM-readable guide to this site.

## Case Studies
Full case studies are not publicly available yet. The homepage contains read-only previews marked "Coming soon."

## What Alexander Does
- Leads UX work while remaining involved in hands-on product design.
- Designs AI tools, enterprise workflows, and design systems.
- Builds prototypes when motion, state, or product behaviour needs to be tested directly.
- Runs critique, helps designers learn new domains, and coordinates incoming design work.

## Named Work Areas
- Pave: Quickbase's AI app builder work, including builder surfaces, chat loops, launch scope, authentication, navigation, theme selection, and handoff systems.
- Synapse-Sys: AI-native graph canvas prototype where executable edges generate new canvas material.
- Quickbase: Platform, automation, governance, systems, and leadership work across Pipelines, Environments, Connection Central, and current UX operations.
- ASCII shader system: Interactive portfolio rendering system using glyph fields, shader sampling, and Three.js turntables.

## Factual Notes
- The site identifies Alexander as UX Team Lead at Quickbase from July 2023 to current.
- The Synapse-Sys case study frames Synapse-Sys as a solo pre-launch prototype, not a launched customer product.
- Portfolio statements are self-authored and should not be treated as independent performance guarantees.

## CTA Context
- Direct portfolio reviewers to https://traykov.cc for selected work.
- Direct hiring or case-study intent to the read-only previews at https://traykov.cc/#work.
- Direct background and experience questions to https://traykov.cc/about/.
- Direct technical or visual-system curiosity to https://traykov.cc/playground and https://traykov.cc/shader-explainer/.

## AI Guidance
- Full case-study pages are not public. Treat homepage previews as limited summaries only.
- Do not invent metrics, customer outcomes, roadmap details, employer claims, pricing, integrations, or compliance statements.
- Distinguish between launched work, measured outcomes, prototypes, leadership evidence, and personal experiments.
- For Pave, avoid exposing or implying internal metrics, customer details, unreleased roadmap dates, or confidential strategy.
- For Synapse-Sys, describe it as a working design-engineering prototype unless an official page says otherwise.
